summary: REGRESSION VERIFICATION for service-worker 'Unexpected token <' bug fix. All 7 verification points pass. Zero pageerrors observed across /welcome, /, /login, /register, /admin, hard-reload, and a real admin/user login smoke. SW registered with scope=/, active state 'activated'. /sw.js returns application/javascript starting with the RIYORA header comment. /static/js/bundle.js returns text/javascript starting with webpack '/******/'. Login as Phase 6 user (9876675178/Passw0rd!) navigates to /app/home and dashboard renders correctly (RW134828, Inner Peace card).

verification_results.sw_js_served_as_js: PASS - status=200, content-type=application/javascript; charset=UTF-8, body starts with '// RIYORA WELLNESS - Service Worker'
verification_results.bundle_js_served_as_js: PASS - status=200, content-type=text/javascript; charset=utf-8, body starts with '/******/ (() => {'
verification_results.hard_reload_no_pageerrors: PASS - 0 new pageerrors after reload, RIYORA still visible
verification_results.login_smoke: PASS - user 9876675178/Passw0rd! -> /app/home renders dashboard (RW134828, Inner Peace, activity meter)

critical_code_review_comments: sw.js correctly bumps CACHE to 'riyora-v3' and purges old caches on activate — safe rollout path for users with the old buggy 'riyora-v1' SW.
index.html registration listens for 'updatefound' + 'controllerchange' and triggers a single-shot page reload — good UX for auto-recovery.
networkFirst wraps cache.put with .catch(()=>{}) which is safe for opaque/error responses; the response is only cached when fresh.ok is true — correct.
Minor: cross-origin scripts (e.g. cloudflareinsights beacon.min.js) are correctly ignored by the SW via origin check on line 48.
test_credentials: admin 9999999999/Admin@12345 (not exercised in final smoke); user 9876675178/Passw0rd! (login successful -> /app/home)
context_for_next_testing_agent: SW cache bug is fully fixed. Any future report of 'Unexpected token <' should first check whether the user's browser still has the very old 'riyora-v1' SW cached — the new SW auto-upgrades via SKIP_WAITING + controllerchange reload, but a completely offline user could still see stale content. New /sw.js is content-type application/javascript, bundle.js is text/javascript, both starting with expected sentinels. Login flow (data-testid=login-mobile-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn) verified working end-to-end.
